question: Which changes occur in persons continuously using internet and mobile phones?
answer:
When someone is constantly on their cell phone, it can cause many health problems.
Tiredness, headaches, trouble sleeping, and confusion, Hearing loss, joint pain, and eyesight issues happen. because of the radiation from cell phones. This is even worse for little kids because the radiation can get through their bones.
When people use the internet for long periods, they feel alone. These kinds of people can't get along with their family members or other people around them.
They tend to become self-centred and selfish. They lose sensitivity towards others.
Such people never take any social responsibility, and the social health is thus disturbed.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Social health

question: Which problems do the common man faces due to incidences of cyber crime?
answer:
Cheaters get their hands on Aadhaar card numbers, PAN card numbers, credit card numbers, and bank card numbers. It is illegal to do this. People can use a PIN number in harmful ways to withdraw money from bank accounts. This is how the thieves take money out of our accounts.
People can be cheated during online shopping.
Someone opens a fake Facebook account and posts false information on it. The girls are mentally and financially abused through these kinds of accounts.
People send rude and offensive messages, obscene pictures, and controversial statements using electronic media in the wrong way.
Through the internet, hackers can send a virus to crash someone’s computer or even their mobile phone.

In all such different ways, common people can be victimised by cybercrime.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Social health

question: Explain the importance of good communication with others.
answer:
Communication is extremely useful for expressing feelings. Different methods of expression include communicating with friends, colleagues, cousins, teachers as well as our parents.
Expressing our feelings through communication with near and dear ones, noting down our feelings, etc. helps us to relieve stress.
Communication also helps to spread knowledge and to build healthy relationships.
Good communication with family members, friends, etc. can help us establish harmonious relationships with others in society.
Communication with others helps to overcome your problems, provide you with moral support, and also get to understand the perspective of people on different issues. 
Good communication skills do not only mean good verbal skills but it also implies one’s listening skills. Apart from being a good orator, it is important that you are a good listener too. One should listen patiently to people around them and help them in the best possible way. 

In other words, we can say that good communication skills result in the all-around development of an individual.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Social health

question: Which are various ways to minimize stress?
answer:
Maintaining good communication and expressing our feelings to friends, peers, cousins, teachers and parents or noting them down in a diary.
Utilizing the free hours in fostering hobbies like material collection, photography, reading, cooking, sculpting, drawing, making rangoli, dancing, singing, listening to music, etc.
Diverting energy and mind towards positive thinking so as to neutralize the negative thoughts. Regular exercising, getting a massage, practicing yoga and meditation.
Laughing loudly so as to relieve stress.
Playing sports and outdoor games so as to involve physical exercise, inculcate discipline, increase interaction with others, drive away loneliness, etc.
Inculcating traits like time management, planning of our own duties and taking own decisions.
If any of the above methods fail to reduce stress and leads to more serious problems like depression and frustration, then medical help, counselling, and/or psychotherapy should be taken.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Social health

question: Give three examples of hobbies to reduce stress.
answer:
To listen to music
Bird watching and nature trails
Reading good books
Playing sports

question: What will you do? Why? You are spending more time in internet/mobile games, phone, etc.
answer:
If I start spending more time on the internet/mobile games, phone, etc. I would follow the steps below:

Designating activities during which the usage of the internet or mobile phones should be strictly avoided. For example, during meals, while studying, etc.
Talking to family members or friends when I feel lonely instead of using the internet or mobile phones. This will reduce the habit of using mobile phones during idle time.
Develop hobbies like reading books, writing a diary, collecting various materials, drawing, singing, listening to music, etc.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Social health

question: What will you do? Why? Child of your neighbour is addicted to tobacco chewing.
answer:
If I find that the child of my neighbour is addicted to tobacco chewing:

I will make him aware about the health problems associated with chewing tobacco like mouth cancer, tooth decay, gum diseases, etc. This will help him to make a decision about quitting tobacco.
I will advise him to reduce the use of tobacco every day till he finally quits it completely.
I will also make him talk to his family or friends regarding the issue, so as to help him get rid of the habit.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Social health

question: What will you do? Why? Your sister has become incommunicative. She prefers to remain alone.
answer:
Feeling lonely or not communicating with others can lead to mental stress. This can eventually affect one’s physical, mental and social health.

I will talk to her and try to find out the reason for her aloofness and the stress. I will discuss her problem and help her find a solution to her problem.
I will try to get her to speak with family members and participate in family discussions and functions. I would also encourage her to pursue her hobbies. This can help her divert her mind from the issues that are troubling her.
If required, I will advise her to consult a professional counsellor and discuss her problems.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Social health

question: What will you do? Why? You have to use free space around your home for good purpose.
answer:
We can build a small yard in the empty land around our house. You can buy garden soil and spread it out in this empty spot. You can put small saplings here and care for them so they grow. This empty room could also be used to start a nursery for young trees.
You can also play games outside in that area. Badminton nets can be set up, and people can play the game in the evenings. Also, the area will be kept clean and free of trash.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Social health

question: What will you do? Why? Your friend has developed the hobby of snapping selfies.
answer:
The bad habit of constantly taking selfies may endanger the life of a person as he is unaware of the risks in his surroundings. 
If I find that my friend has developed the hobby of snapping selfies:

I will warn him to be careful when taking selfies and to stay aware of his surroundings to avoid accidents.
I will try to persuade him to avoid taking selfies on every occasion and instead enjoy the moment with friends and/or family.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Social health

question: What will you do? Why? Your brother studying in XII has developed the stress.
answer:
There are various reasons because of which my brother may have developed stress.
The most possible reason is that he is in class XII and is worried about his board exams and his future.
However, my first step would be to sit down with him and understand the cause of his stress.
After understanding the cause, I would work with him to chalk out a strategy to solve his problem.
I would talk to him in a way that encourages him to think positively and helps divert his mind from negative thoughts.
Another strategy would be to suggest he indulge in his favourite activities.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Social health

question: What type of changes occurs in a home having chronically ill old person? How will you help to maintain good atmosphere?
answer:
Having chronically ill old patients in a family affects the physical and mental state of other members of the family as well.
It is not easy to handle old age people and if they are ill, it becomes all the more difficult.
It not only affects the family monetarily but also drains them physically and emotionally, as it is not easy to see the suffering of your loved ones.
Since it is a tough situation, it is important that each person in the family stays patient and stays calm. They need to remain positive and ward off negative feelings and thoughts.
They should try to talk to the patient and share their entire day with them, even if the patient is not responsive. Just being present around them helps protect them from loneliness.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Social health

question: Enlist various factors affecting the social health in your residential area. Decide the necessary changes to correct the situation and implement those changes.
answer:
Various factors affecting social health in our residential area include:

Economic conditions of the people.
Lack of resources for education.
Social status and
Barriers between people due to the caste system lead to stressful circumstances.

Ways to implement to correct the situation are:

Proper camps should be installed in such places.
Regular updates on the development should be provided by the government.
Funds allocated for rural development should not go into corrupt hands.
People need to know about these issues through nukkad nataks, protests, speeches, campaigns, NGOs, and other events.
They should have access to all the tools and resources they need, such as schools, colleges, hospitals, and so on.
An important part is education. These places should have government schools and colleges where people can learn about social issues. There should be campaigns to raise awareness of how important schooling is.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Social health

question: Complete the table. (Motor accident, land sliding, forest fire, theft, riot, war, epidemic, drought, locust attack, financial crisis, flood, famine) Disaster Symptoms Effects Remedy
answer:
Disaster
Symptoms
Effects
Remedy

Motor accident
Rash driving, drink and drive, and ignorance of safety rules
Loss of life, loss of property
Following traffic rules, avoid driving if tipsy

Land sliding
New cracks or bulges in the ground, street or sidewalks, sunken road beds
Loss of property, loss of life, adverse impact on the environment
Give help to clear debris, call an ambulance to send victims to hospitals

Forest fire
Lightning, human activities involving smoke and fire near forest areas
Impact on the environment, loss of lives of animals
Call the fire brigade, give first aid to the animals which survived the burns

Theft
Sheer carelessness towards property and wealth
Tension and loss of property
Informing police, giving emotional support

Riot
Opinion differences, political influence
Loss of life and property
Give shelter to innocent victims, provide food and clothing and first-aid to the victims

War
Transgressing opinion differences between countries 
Loss of life and property, Impact on the country and its economy
Give shelter to innocent victims, provide food, clothing and first-aid to the victims

Epidemic
Spreading of viruses from different animals
Loss of life, health risks, and impact on the environment 
Give proper medication to people, preparing antidotes against epidemics

Drought
No rainfall for a long duration
Food and water crisis, loss of life

Locust attack
Leaf distortion, chlorosis, yellow to brown spots, premature leaf drop
Crop destruction, scarcity in food
Spraying pesticides

Financial crisis
Bankruptcy, loss of business or services in an individual case
Impact on the country and its economy, impact on the individual
Giving support to friends and family, and asking for support from other countries when crises happen in a country

 Flood
Cloudburst, heavy rainfall
Health risks, loss of property, food and water crisis, loss of life
Donate food and clothing for flood victims, stay at some height if possible 

 Famine
Heavy rainfall, drought
Food and water crisis, displacement of people, loss of life
Donate food and water for victims, provide shelter and monetary help
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Disaster Management

question: Write note. Disaster management Authority
answer: The Disaster Management Authority is responsible for developing policies, plans, and guidelines for disaster management to ensure a timely and effective response to disasters. This authority has been established at the level of government. The following flow chart indicates the function of control and coordination under the disaster management from the national to the village level. The Disaster Management Act, 2005, has been passed in our country.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Disaster Management

question: Write a note. Nature of disaster management
answer:
Disaster management is the strategy and course of action to be executed at the time of any such disaster to save as much life as possible. It can be divided into two parts:

Pre-disaster Management:
Pre-disaster management phase is concerned with the complete preparation and planning in order to enable us to face any kind of situation.

The first step includes identifying the pre-disaster areas.
The second step includes collecting information about intensity of disaster and probable sites of disasters using predictive intensity maps and hazard maps.
Next step is to get training for disaster management.
Another important aspect of pre-disaster management is spreading awareness among the common people through various awareness programmes.

Post-disaster Management:
Post-disaster management is concerned with the situation that arise after a disaster has occurred. It includes the following components:

The primary concern is to provide help to victims preferably with the participation of local people.
Establishment of help centres to provide all kind of possible help required by the people.
Categorisation of help materials that are received from the help centres and distributing them efficiently to the people affected by the disaster.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Disaster Management

question: Write a note. Mock drill
answer:
mock drill is a practice to assess preparedness for dealing with a sudden disaster or attack.
For this purpose, virtual or apparent situations that simulate the disaster are created.
It checks how quickly people can respond to any kind of disaster. Trained personnel are present to monitor the emergency plans being carried out.
People also understand their responsibilities during an actual disaster.
The experts also assess the execution of the disaster-response plan.
Through such mock drills, the system’s efficacy can be assessed. In the future, when a calamity strikes, there will already be disaster-response preparations in place. Therefore, a mock drill is useful.
E.g. In the event of a fire, the school can conduct a mock drill with firefighters.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Disaster Management

question: Write a note. Disaster Management Act, 2005
answer:
The Disaster Management Act, 2005, was passed by the Rajya Sabha on 28 November and by the Lok Sabha on 12 December 2005.
It received the approval from the President of India on 9 January 2006.
This act has 11 chapters and 79 sections.
The act extends to the whole of India. This act called for the formation of National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) with prime minister of India as its chair person.
The Act under Section 8 encouraged the central government to constitute a National Executive Committee (NEC) to assist the National Authority.
All State Governments are mandated under Section 14 of the Act to establish a State Disaster Management Authority (SDMA). Section 42 of the act calls for the establishment of a National Institute of Disaster Management.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Disaster Management

question: Explain the role of district disaster control unit after occurrence of any disaster.
answer:
The district control unit oversees disaster management in the district. It is formed immediately either after a disaster or when a warning is given about an upcoming one.
The review of various aspects of disasters is done by the district control unit.
Through the disaster control unit, continuous contact is maintained with various agencies such as the army, air force, navy, telecommunications department, and paramilitary forces for obtaining help.
The unit also coordinates with various voluntary organizations for their help in disaster management.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Disaster Management

question: Give the reasons for increase in human disasters after the World War-II.
answer:
fter the Second World War, the sense of peace and brotherhood among global citizens was lost. The geographic, religious, racial, and ethnic differences increased significantly.
The atrocities that Nazi has performed made a deep impact on the minds of people. Terrorism, abduction, robberies and social unrest increased in almost all countries.
The financial losses incurred during World War II. The misuse of science and technology was used to address these deficits.
At the end of World War II, the atomic bombs were dropped on Japan. This has created health problems worldwide.
Social inequality, economic disparity, and racial and religious differences were some adversaries that created unrest in the country.
Later, the neighbouring nations kept on fighting. The geographical boundaries were changed. People have always felt insecure. The terrorism flourished. All such instances gave rise to man-made disasters.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Disaster Management

question: Which are the objectives of disaster management?
answer:
To save human life from disasters. To help them move away from the place of disasters by rapid action.
To supply essential commodities to the affected people. This helps to reduce the gravity of the disaster. People are provided with grains, water, clothing, and other basic necessities under this objective.
To restore the conditions of affected people to normal.
To restore the conditions of affected people to normal.
To plan and carry out safety steps so that people can learn how to deal with disasters in the future.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Disaster Management

question: Why is it essential to get the training of first aid?
answer:
It enables a person to use appropriate preventive measures to save lives during an emergency.
It enables the person to provide primary care and medications to victims before formal medical care.
It enables the person to prevent further damage to the victims.
It helps the person remain relaxed and calm in critical situations.
It enables the person to give comfort and care to the victim.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Disaster Management

question: Which different methods are used for transportation of patients? Why?
answer:
Cradle method: useful for children and underweight victims.
Carrying piggy back: useful for carrying patients who are unconscious.
Human crutch method: If one leg of the person is injured, the victim is supported with minimal load on the other leg. This is called the human crutch method.
Carrying on two hands chair: Useful for those patients who cannot use their hands but can hold their body upright.
Pulling or lifting method: Useful for carrying an unconscious patient over a short distance.
Carrying on a four-hand chair: Useful when support is needed for the part below the waist.
Stretcher: By making a temporary stretcher in case of an emergency, the unconscious patient can be moved. Such temporary stretchers are made by using bamboo, blankets, etc.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Disaster Management

question: Write down the reasons, effects and remedial measures taken for any two disasters experienced by you.

Motor accident:

Reason: Rash driving and ignorance of safety rules.
Effects: Injuries to oneself and family, as well as damage to property.
Remedial measures: Giving emotional support to victims, calling an ambulance and taking the victims to the hospital.

Theft:

Reason: Sheer carelessness towards property and wealth.
Effects: Loss of life and property.
Remedial measures: Giving emotional support to victims, calling the police to register the case.

question: Which different aspects of disaster management would you check for your school? Why?
answer:
For the pre-disaster management at school, the following aspects would be inspected.

Are the telephones in the school working properly?
Is there a first-aid box in each class?
Are there any basic medicines in the school?
Is the team ready to rescue younger children from lower classes?
Has a monitor or prefect participated in a mock drill? Does he/she know about first aid?
Is the parent representative’s contact available in emergency situations?
Is the Medical Officer/Doctor present on the school campus?
Is there enough drinking water and some dry snacks available in the school?
Are the staircases and corridors suitable for the quick evacuation of the children?

question: Explain the symbol.
answer:
This symbol represents toxic substances, which are harmful even on a single exposure. The action of such substances is rapid and can be fatal.
The disaster associated with this symbol is an unpredictable or intentional health disaster. Exposure to radiation, certain chemicals, and poisonous gases is an example of a toxic substance.

They should be used only in smart, limited ways.

answer: The given symbol represents oxidising substances. Some chemical compounds exhibit oxidising properties. They execute chemical processes with remarkable rapidity. The fabric’s propensity to facilitate reactions may result in combustion. Therefore, oxidising agents must be managed with caution.

question: Explain that why is it said like that? Effective disaster management makes us well prepared for future.
answer:
Disaster can strike at any time. Sudden disasters can be man-made with malicious intent or accidental.
When a natural disaster occurs abruptly and with significant force, extensive property destruction and environmental degradation ensue, accompanied by considerable loss of human and animal life.
Therefore, it is essential to be equipped to mitigate the effects of potential future disasters.
While we cannot prevent natural disasters, we can significantly mitigate their adverse impacts by implementing a disaster management plan.

question: Following is the picture of disaster. How will be your pre-and post-disaster management in case you face this disaster?
answer:
The picture shows a gas leak from an LPG cylinder.
Pre-disaster management:

The gas cylinder and the rubber tubes used must be certified.
Gas cylinders must be properly checked for leakage from the pipe nozzle or any other part while installing.

Post-disaster management:

Do not try to turn ON or turn OFF any electrical appliances in the surrounding area.
Put out all flames, incense sticks, etc.
Turn off the LPG regulator and immediately put on the safety cap.
Open all the windows and doors to ensure ventilation.
Get in touch with the LPG cylinder dealer immediately.
subject: Biology
chapter: Science and Technology 2 [English] Standard 10 Disaster Management

question: Following is the picture of disaster. How will be your pre-and post-disaster management in case you face this disaster?

The picture shows a cyclone.

Keep an emergency kit. This kit should include portable lights, medications, etc.
